codeReview.summary: The original repeat-use feature introduced favorite quick-open by routing favorite chips directly into `onOpenFavoriteHost`, which bypassed the existing host repair gate and caused broken favorites to dead-end in session connect failures. The fix commit closes that gap by applying the same `needsRepair` check used by host rows before favorite quick-open, so blocked or missing linked identities now reopen the host editor/repair flow while healthy favorites still quick-open by saved host ID.

summary: Reviewed the prior failure, both feature handoffs, both transcript skeletons, architecture/mission guidance, and the original and fix diffs. The rerun fix adequately resolves the favorite-host repairability regression: favorite discovery chips now follow the same repair/edit gate as host rows, preserve auth-family repair context through `HostEditorDraft.from(...)`, and add focused instrumentation coverage for both healthy and broken favorite quick-open paths.
